risk_if_not_implemented: Without Dual Authorization for Physical Access, unauthorized users may gain access to systems or data, increasing risk of data breaches and insider threats.

compensating_control_1.name: Access Enforcement
compensating_control_1.description: Mechanisms exist to enforce Logical Access Control (LAC) permissions that conform to the principle of "least privilege."
compensating_control_1.justification: Access Enforcement (IAC-20) provides access control enforcement that compensates for the absence of Dual Authorization for Physical Access (PES-02.2) by restricting system and data access to authorized users through alternative identity and access mechanisms. Given the process-oriented nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.

compensating_control_2.name: Physical Access Authorizations
compensating_control_2.description: Physical access control mechanisms exist to maintain a current list of personnel with authorized access to organizational facilities (except for those areas within the facility officially designated as publicly accessible).
compensating_control_2.justification: Physical Access Authorizations (PES-02) provides access control enforcement that compensates for the absence of Dual Authorization for Physical Access (PES-02.2) by restricting system and data access to authorized users through alternative identity and access mechanisms. Given the process-oriented nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.
